title: Gritty Mexican Corn Pozole
description: A savory Jalisco-style pozole with a Chinese sesame sauce twist, topped with pickled nuka cucumber and tofu for a vegetarian-friendly cozy bowl.
instructions: Rinse hominy corn and add to a large pot with 6 cups water, bring to a boil, then simmer for 30 minutes until tender.
Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, and lime juice until well combined to make the sauce.
Stir masa harina into the simmering hominy, whisking constantly to avoid lumps, and cook for 5 minutes until the mixture thickens.
Add the prepared sesame sauce to the pot and stir thoroughly to coat the corn.
Reduce heat to low and let the pozole simmer for 20 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
After simmering, stir in diced tofu and cook for an additional 5 minutes until tofu is heated through.
Remove pot from heat, then stir in nuka-pickle and chopped scallions.
Taste the pozole and add a pinch of sea salt if needed.
Transfer the pozole onto a wooden board for serving, ensuring it stays warm.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with extra scallions or lime wedges if desired.
